title: task
description:
python/src/lichen/schc/reassembly.py:96 - The FragmentReceiver.receive() method computes pos = window_size - 1 - fcn without validating that fcn < window_size (for non-ALL_1 fragments). Out-of-range FCN values cause negative position calculations, leading to tiles being stored at negative indices in the _tiles dict.

Failure scenario: A malicious sender crafts fragments with large FCN values (e.g., fcn=10 with window_size=3). The receiver stores these at negative indices like -8. While the contiguity check prevents corrupted output, the garbage tiles waste memory and could enable DoS by filling the receiver's memory with useless entries.

Fix: Before line 97, validate fcn < self.window_size and discard/ignore fragments with invalid FCN values. Example: if frag.fcn >= self.window_size: return ReceiverResult()
